title: Fix Codex 5h menu bar showing 0%: switch to live ChatGPT /wham/usage API
description: CodexUsageService reads stale ~/.codex/sessions rollout files; user burns ChatGPT quota via OpenCode so files are stale and window() forces 0% on expired reset. Replace with live GET https://chatgpt.com/backend-api/wham/usage using OAuth token from ~/.codex/auth.json, mirroring ClaudeAPIService. In-memory token refresh only; return nil on failure (never fabricate 0%). Scope: 5h+weekly windows only.
close_reason: Replaced stale rollout-file parsing with live /wham/usage fetch; 6 tests pass, live value 25% (was 0%)

title: Fix Codex limit refresh
description: OpenAI/Codex rate limits are reported as not refreshing. Diagnose the limit refresh path, fix the stale refresh behavior, and verify with tests or build.
close_reason: Expired Codex rate-limit windows now refresh to zero usage and roll their reset timestamp forward; added focused Codex usage service tests. App build succeeds.

title: Fix synced usage pricing
description: Blog/session usage rows currently sync with missing or stale cost_usd values because pricing is not computed consistently for provider/model pairs. Add provider-aware pricing for known OpenAI and Anthropic models, including Codex auto-review per OpenAI docs, and cover it with regression tests.
close_reason: Resolved: blog/session usage aggregation now computes provider-aware cost_usd via ModelPricing for Anthropic and OpenAI models, with regression coverage for Codex auto-review, GPT 5.5 fast, Anthropic pricing, and unknown model behavior.

title: ClaudeMeterTests fails to compile: OutageTrackingTests references missing symbols
description: OutageTrackingTests.swift references symbols that do not exist in the codebase, breaking the entire ClaudeMeterTests target compilation (so no tests can run). Missing: UsageViewModel.activeClaudeIncident, UsageViewModel.isClaudeServiceDown, MockAPIService.setMockError, MockAPIService.setMockSnapshot, MockCredentialProvider.configure. Either the outage-tracking feature/mocks were removed/renamed and the test wasn't updated, or the test was added ahead of the implementation. Pre-existing; discovered while fixing ClaudeMeter-9bl.

title: Fix recurring macOS keychain authorization prompt on launch
description: macOS app shows a keychain authorization prompt for com.tartinerlabs.ClaudeMeter on every launch/refresh. Cause: MacOSCredentialService.loadCredentials() calls syncToKeychain() which writes to ClaudeMeter's own keychain via SecItemUpdate/Add. Nothing on macOS reads that item back (iCloud sync disabled), so the write is dead weight that only triggers the ACL prompt. Fix: remove the syncToKeychain call and the unused method.
close_reason: Removed syncToKeychain call and method from MacOSCredentialService; macOS no longer writes to its own keychain, eliminating the per-launch authorization prompt. Build succeeds.

title: Show Codex 5-hour limit in menu bar
description: The menu bar Codex indicator should display the 5-hour Codex quota window instead of the weekly window when both are available.
close_reason: Menu bar Codex indicator now prefers the 5-hour Codex quota window.

title: Verify OpenCode Go usage and limit behavior
description: Confirm how OpenCode Go usage and limits should be surfaced by comparing ClaudeMeter against public implementations and available OpenCode data sources. Adjust code if OpenCode Go exposes refreshable quota windows or if current usage parsing is incomplete.
close_reason: Verified OpenCode Go dashboard quota behavior and added optional dashboard parser/service integration.

title: Verify Codex limit refresh behavior against other tools
description: Compare ClaudeMeter Codex rate-limit refresh behavior against CodexBar, OpenUsage, and other public implementations to confirm expired-window handling is correct. Adjust implementation if needed.
close_reason: Compared against CodexBar, OpenUsage, upstream Codex, abtop, and local Codex rollout logs. Updated parser to map windows by window_minutes, support reset_at/reset_after_seconds variants, and added coverage for these cases. App build succeeds; test target remains blocked by stale OutageTrackingTests.

title: Repair stale outage tracking tests
description: The macOS test target fails to compile because OutageTrackingTests.swift references removed UsageViewModel outage APIs and mock helper methods. Update or remove these stale tests so focused test runs can execute again.

title: Fix stale Credentials card in macOS Settings (file → Keychain)
description: Anthropic dropped the ~/.claude/.credentials.json file in favor of the macOS Keychain entry 'Claude Code-credentials'. MacOSCredentialService already reads from Keychain, but SettingsTabView still checks for the file (credentialsFound via FileManager.fileExists), shows the dead file path, and has a non-working reveal-in-Finder button. Result: card shows red 'Not found' even though credentials load fine. Fix: check Keychain for status, relabel source to Keychain, remove folder button.

title: OpenUsage-style detail UI: sidebar + per-provider pages (trend, per-model)
description: Rebuild popover like OpenUsage: left icon rail (overview + per-provider pages), ProviderDetailView with Status/Console links, window rows, Today/Yesterday/30d cost, usage-trend sparkline, per-model breakdown. Shared with dashboard. Spark/Credits omitted (not in local logs). Adds ProviderDetail model, daily-cost queries, SparklineView bars.
close_reason: OpenUsage-style sidebar + ProviderDetailView shipped (popover + dashboard): rail switches overview/per-provider; detail shows links, windows w/ status dots, Today/Yesterday/30d, usage-trend sparkline (bars), per-model %. Builds clean (macOS+widgets). Spark/Credits omitted (not in local logs).

title: P6: Tests & verification (multi-provider)
description: Unit tests: CodexLogSource fixture (token+window mapping), OpenCodeLogSource fixture SQLite, aggregator dedup+cost, TokenLogEntry migration. Build macOS scheme. Manual run cross-checking Codex/OpenCode totals vs raw data. Fix ClaudeMeter-6rc (test target build break) first.

title: P4: Provider selection plumbing (macOS, minimal)
description: enabledProviders:Set<Provider> in UserDefaults with auto-detect by probing paths/DB. DependencyContainer.createTokenUsageService(enabledProviders:) + createCodexUsageService(). UsageViewModel new fields. Claude stays default.
notes: Auto-detect wiring done (DependencyContainer probes Codex/OpenCode paths; VM injects CodexUsageService). Remaining: explicit enabledProviders Set<Provider> in Settings UI to toggle providers on/off.
close_reason: Auto-detect wiring + Codex menu-bar toggle done; full enable/disable settings optional.

title: P3: Codex rate-limit windows (CodexUsageService, macOS)
description: CodexUsageService reads newest rollout file, latest token_count event with rate_limits → ProviderUsageSnapshot(provider:.codex) with primary→.codexFiveHour, secondary→.codexWeekly (used_percent/resets_at), planName=plan_type.
close_reason: CodexUsageService reads 5h/weekly rate-limit windows from newest rollout (used_percent/resets_at/plan_type) → ProviderUsageSnapshot. Verified vs real data: plan prolite, 5h 51%, 7d 20%. macOS builds clean.

title: P2: Multi-provider token/cost (UsageLogSource aggregator, macOS)
description: New UsageLogSource protocol + ProviderUsageEntry. Implement ClaudeLogSource (refactor existing JSONL parser), CodexLogSource (~/.codex/sessions rollout JSONL, map total_token_usage), OpenCodeLogSource (SQLite session table read-only, compute cost via ModelPricing since cost col=0). TokenUsageService becomes aggregator; TokenUsageSnapshot.byProvider. TokenLogEntry gains provider field + composite-id migration. Constants: codex/opencode paths.
close_reason: Live multi-provider token/cost aggregator shipped (re-confirming closed).

title: P1: Provider foundation (ClaudeMeterKit)
description: Add Provider enum (claude/codex/openCode) with capabilities (.rateWindows/.tokenCost) and pricingProviderKey. Extend UsageWindowType with .codexFiveHour (5h)/.codexWeekly (7d). Add ProviderUsageSnapshot {provider, windows:[UsageWindow], extraUsage?, planName?, fetchedAt} + adapter from Claude UsageSnapshot. Add TokenCount.reasoningTokens (default 0).
close_reason: Provider enum, UsageWindowType codex cases, ProviderUsageSnapshot, TokenCount.reasoningTokens implemented; Kit builds clean

title: Multi-provider support (Codex CLI + OpenCode)
description: Expand ClaudeMeter beyond Claude to monitor OpenAI Codex CLI and OpenCode. Token/cost everywhere + rate-limit windows where exposed (Codex has them in rollout logs; OpenCode is token/cost only via SQLite). Provider-agnostic data layer; UI integration deferred until single-active vs all-at-once decided. See plan: ~/.claude/plans/remember-i-want-to-validated-unicorn.md

title: Align blog usage sync with blog ingest contract
description: Update the macOS blog usage sync to match the blog repository ingest contract for Claude, Codex, and OpenCode Go: preserve opencode as the agent, carry opencode-go as provider, parse OpenCode nested timestamps, update coverage tests, and clarify settings copy.
notes:
Implementation complete: aligned blog usage sync with the blog ingest contract. OpenCode remains agent=opencode, providerID such as opencode-go is preserved, nested OpenCode time.created timestamps are parsed, combined source coverage was added, and settings copy now names Claude/Codex/OpenCode Go. Validation: macOS app build succeeds. Targeted test execution remains blocked by pre-existing OutageTrackingTests compile errors tracked separately in ClaudeMeter-6rc. Attempted bd close but bd failed with: column depends_on_id could not be found in any table in scope.
Closed via bd update --status closed because bd close fails in embedded mode dependency blocker check: column depends_on_id could not be found in any table in scope.
close_reason: Aligned blog usage sync with the blog ingest contract: OpenCode remains agent=opencode, providerID such as opencode-go is preserved, nested OpenCode time.created timestamps are parsed, combined source coverage was added, and settings copy now names Claude/Codex/OpenCode Go. macOS app build succeeds. Targeted test execution remains blocked by pre-existing OutageTrackingTests compile errors tracked separately in ClaudeMeter-6rc.

title: ClaudeMeterTests target broken: OutageTrackingTests references nonexistent members
description: OutageTrackingTests.swift references UsageViewModel.isOutageError/activeClaudeIncident/isClaudeServiceDown and MockAPIService.setMockError/setMockSnapshot, MockCredentialProvider.configure — none defined in the repo. Pre-existing on main (verified via clean-tree build-for-testing). Blocks running the entire test suite. Likely an outage-tracking feature whose impl/mocks were never merged (or the test was left behind).

title: Drop ccusage backend; make native JSONL parser the sole token-usage source
description: ccusage numbers disagree with the native parser (unscoped/all-agents + LiteLLM pricing vs Claude-only + hardcoded Anthropic pricing). Remove CCUsageRunnerService + tests + wiring and the now-dead multi-agent plumbing (UsageProvider, ProviderUsageSummary, TokenUsageDataSource, snapshot provider fields). Delete scripts/compare_usage.swift. Blog sync unaffected (independent parser).
notes: Done: deleted CCUsageRunnerService.swift + tests + scripts/compare_usage.swift; removed ccusage wiring from UsageViewModel & DependencyContainer; removed dead multi-agent plumbing (UsageProvider, ProviderUsageSummary, TokenUsageDataSource, snapshot provider fields) from TokenUsage.swift. macOS app BUILD SUCCEEDED. Test suite could NOT run due to pre-existing OutageTrackingTests breakage (filed separately) — unrelated to this change. Not committed (conservative profile).
close_reason: Resolved: ccusage backend, related wiring, dead multi-agent plumbing, compare_usage.swift script, and stale README documentation are removed; app code no longer references ccusage.

title: Correct 0.11.1 release notes
description: Replace the incorrect archive wording in the 0.11.1 changelog and live GitHub release body with the intended maintenance-release note for release metadata and Sparkle appcast notes.

title: Clean up historical release metadata
description: Restore missing changelog and appcast release-note metadata, recover available GitHub release assets from duplicate drafts, and verify the release inventory remains consistent.
notes: Completed release metadata cleanup: restored changelog 0.10.8, repaired recoverable release assets, removed duplicate drafts, and fixed v0.11.0 release/appcast notes.

title: Add unscoped ccusage backend
description: Implement a macOS-only unscoped ccusage local usage backend with pnx/pnpx/pnpm dlx runner fallback, preserving provider identity for future multi-agent UI while keeping native Claude parsing as fallback.

title: Reduce blog usage sync throttle
description: The passive blog usage sync currently waits about one hour between attempts. Reduce the throttle aggressively while keeping manual sync bypass behavior and tests aligned.

title: Add passive blog usage sync
description: Implement macOS-only passive sync from local Claude, Codex, and OpenCode usage sources into the blog usage ingest endpoint, with Keychain token storage, Settings controls, hourly throttle, and tests.

title: Codex: surface Reviews, per-model, and Credits windows from /wham/usage
description: Deferred from ClaudeMeter-5ax (live Codex usage). The /wham/usage response also returns code_review_rate_limit (Reviews), additional_rate_limits[] (per-model GPT-5.x windows with limit_name/limit_window_seconds), and credits.balance. Surfacing these needs new UsageWindowType cases + menu/detail UI, matching OpenUsage's codex plugin detail lines. Out of scope for the 0% bug fix.

title: Make OpenCode Go dashboard auth setup discoverable
description: ClaudeMeter now supports OpenCode Go quota windows when dashboard workspace/cookie config is present, but there is no in-app guidance or browser-cookie discovery. Add settings/help text or a setup flow for OPENCODE_GO_WORKSPACE_ID and OPENCODE_GO_AUTH_COOKIE / config-file based setup.

title: Dynamic popover height per selected tab
description: The menu-bar popover currently uses a fixed 372x560 frame (MenuBarView). When switching rail tabs (overview vs each provider), the height should adapt to the selected page's content instead of staying fixed — short pages (e.g. OpenCode) shouldn't show empty space, tall pages should grow up to a max cap (then scroll). Implement size-to-content with a max height; animate height changes on tab switch. Related to ClaudeMeter-1ez (footer + sizing polish).

title: Use real brand logos for providers (Claude, OpenAI/Codex, OpenCode)
description:
Replace the SF Symbol placeholders in Provider.iconName (sparkles / chevron.left.forwardslash.chevron.right / curlybraces) with actual brand logos for Claude, OpenAI (Codex), and OpenCode.

Scope:
- Add logo assets to Assets.xcassets (vector PDF/SVG preferred; provide light/dark variants; consider a monochrome/template version for the menu-bar label which is rendered via ImageRenderer in MenuBarIconView).
- Extend Provider (ClaudeMeterKit) to expose an asset/image name (keep SF Symbol as fallback) or add a small ProviderLogo view.
- Update consumers to use the logo instead of Image(systemName:): ProviderCardView header, ProviderDetailView header, MenuBarView rail tabs, MenuBarIconView (menu-bar label), DashboardTabView.

Considerations:
- Full-color logos in the monochrome menu-bar may need a template/tinted variant.
- Trademark/brand-guideline usage for third-party logos (Anthropic/OpenAI/OpenCode) — verify acceptable use before shipping.
- Keep accentColor as-is for tints/backgrounds.

title: Codex Spark/Spark-Weekly windows + Credits bar when available
description: Surface Codex 'Spark'/'Spark Weekly' rate-limit windows and the Credits bar when the data is present. Not in current local rollout logs (only primary 5h + secondary 7d + plan_type, credits null) — likely needs the OpenAI account/usage API or richer log fields. Render in ProviderDetailView when non-null.

title: Footer 'Next update in Xm' countdown + popover sizing polish
description: Add an OpenUsage-style 'Next update in Xm' countdown in the popover footer (derive from lastRefresh + refreshInterval). Polish the panel: size-to-content with a max cap instead of the fixed 372x560, tune spacing/typography to match OpenUsage density.

title: Provider enable/disable settings panel
description: Settings UI to toggle which providers (Claude/Codex/OpenCode) are tracked/shown, replacing pure auto-detect. Implements the full enabledProviders Set<Provider> persisted to UserDefaults; DependencyContainer + ViewModel honor it; rail/cards hide disabled providers.

title: Multi-provider UI for iOS dashboard and widgets/Live Activity
description: macOS popover + dashboard now show Claude/Codex/OpenCode via ProviderCardView. Remaining surfaces: iOS DashboardView, Home Screen widgets, Lock Screen widget, Live Activity (MetricType is Claude-shaped). Note: Codex/OpenCode data sources are macOS-only (local logs), so iOS would need credential/data sharing first.

title: Persist Codex/OpenCode entries to SwiftData (provider field + multi-source import)
description: P2 delivered the live computeSnapshot multi-provider path. Remaining: add provider field to TokenLogEntry (+composite-id migration) and feed Codex/OpenCode sources into the TokenUsageRepository import path so historical/Wrapped data includes non-Claude providers. Live dashboard already shows all providers without this.

title: P5: Multi-provider UI integration (DEFERRED)
description: Gated on user decision: single-active vs all-at-once. Wire dashboard/menu-bar/widgets/notifications for multiple providers. App rename out of scope (track separately). Do NOT start until UI direction chosen.
notes: macOS Dashboard now shows Codex (5h/weekly windows + plan) and Other Providers cost (OpenCode). Remaining: menu-bar popover, widgets/Live Activity, iOS dashboard, and per-provider notification toggles. Decision single-active vs all-at-once still open for those surfaces (dashboard uses all-at-once sections).
close_reason: OpenUsage-style provider cards shipped on macOS (popover + dashboard). iOS/widgets → ClaudeMeter-d01.
